etymology.description: 䰜 (cauldron) + 米 (rice) = cauldron cooking rice -> congee
etymology.interpretation: The character 鬻(yù, 'congee') combines a cauldron 䰜 (cauldron) with 米 (rice) to depict cooking congee, capturing the essential concept of rice cooked in a cauldron. The two 弓 (gōng, 'bow') represent the steam rising, completing the image of cooking. Initially, 鬻 was used for 'congee,' but later took on the phonetic value for 'to sell' through borrowing.
